pattern: Two min-heaps guarding the front and back candidate windows let each hiring round pick the globally cheapest worker with deterministic tie-breaking while the windows advance inward.
hints: Each round you only ever consider the cheapest of the first or last candidates, so keep both ends in priority queues.
After hiring from one end, pull a fresh worker from the still-untouched middle into that same end's queue if any remain.
Seed a front heap with the first c workers and a back heap with the last c, hire k times always taking the smaller top (front wins ties), and refill from a shrinking middle pointer.

edge_inputs.description: windows overlap and a worker is shared
candidates cover the whole array, no refills
tie broken toward the lower index
candidates exceeds half so windows immediately overlap
hire every worker in order of refills

stress.description: large cost array with small candidate windows and many rounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    costs = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    candidates = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [costs, k, candidates]
stress.note: random k and candidates exercise refill and overlap paths

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def totalCost(self, costs, k, candidates):
        a = list(costs)
        total = 0
        for _ in range(k):
            n = len(a)
            best_val = None
            best_idx = -1
            front = list(range(min(candidates, n)))
            back = list(range(max(0, n - candidates), n))
            pool = sorted(set(front + back))
            for idx in pool:
                if best_val is None or a[idx] < best_val:
                    best_val = a[idx]
                    best_idx = idx
            total += best_val
            a.pop(best_idx)
        return total
